revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:泉殿宮]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Izudono-gū Shrine | location = {{ill|Suita|en|Suita|qid=Q653510|lt=Suita, Osaka|1=Suita}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.76083|N|135.51725|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
{{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11088805}}
{{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja|住吉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1114013}} | Status = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | Annual Festival = May 3 | Chief Priest = Miyawaki family | image = File:Izudonogu.jpg | location = {{ill|Suita|qid=Q653510|1=Suita}} }} '''Izudono-gū Shrine''' (泉殿宮, Izudonogu) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Suita|en|Suita|qid=Q653510|lt=Suita, Osaka|1=Suita}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. == History == Originally, the shrine '''enshrined only [[Ukanomitama]]''' (宇迦之御魂大神). However, in '''869 (Jōgan 11),''' during a prolonged '''drought in the region''', a '''portable shrine (神輿, mikoshi) of Susanoo-no-Mikoto (建速須佐之男大神)'''—destined for enshrinement in '''Heian-kyō (Kyoto) as a guardian deity'''—was temporarily stationed at Izudono-gū Shrine. The '''local villagers''' prayed for rain, and it is said that '''a sudden shower saved them from the drought'''. Since then, '''[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] was enshrined alongside Ukanomitama'''. It is also said that '''a sacred spring emerged on the shrine grounds''' at that time. In '''1889 (Meiji 22),''' during a '''brewing technology exchange in Munich, Germany''', '''the spring water from Izudono-gū Shrine was declared ideal for beer brewing'''. As a result, '''Osaka Beer Company (now {{ill|Asahi Breweries|en|Asahi Breweries|qid=Q720479|lt=Asahi Breweries|1=Asahi Breweries}}) established a brewery near the shrine'''. Unfortunately, '''the spring dried up in the 1950s''', but the '''"Izudono Sacred Spring" (泉殿霊泉)''' remains as a relic of the historic water source. === Chief Priests === The '''Miyawaki family''', descendants of the {{ill|Fujiwara Nan-ke|en|Fujiwara Nan-ke|qid=Q2975332|lt=Southern Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara Nan-ke}}, has traditionally served as the shrine's priests (社家, shake). However, during the {{ill|Ōshio Heihachirō Rebellion|ja|大塩平八郎の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōshio Heihachirō Rebellion|1=Rebellion of Ōshio Heihachirō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4126363}} in '''1837 (Tenpō 8),''' the '''then-chief priest, Miyawaki Shima (宮脇志摩), was suspected of being involved in the rebellion because he was the uncle of Ōshio Heihachirō'''. As a result, '''he took his own life''', his '''male descendants were exiled''', and the '''Miyawaki family lost its hereditary position'''. Nearly '''30 years later, during the Meiji Restoration, the family was reinstated''', and the Miyawaki family once again resumed the '''role of chief priest''' at Izudono-gū Shrine. [[File:Spring ruins.JPG|thumbnail|240px|Izudono Sacred Spring]] == Cultural Properties == ; Suita City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property * '''Izudono-gū Shrine Kagura Shishi (神楽獅子, Sacred Lion Dance)''' == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival (歳旦祭)''' – January 1 * '''Tōka Ebisu (十日戎, Ebisu Festival)''' – January 9–11 * '''Kinensai (祈念祭, Inari Festival)''' – April 3 * '''Annual Festival (festival, Spring Festival)''' – May 3 * '''Nagoshi no Harae (夏越祓, Summer Purification Ritual)''' – June 30 * '''O-mizu Matsuri (お水祭, Summer Water Festival)''' – August 3 * '''Niinamesai (新嘗祭, Harvest Festival)''' – October 3 * '''Ōharae (大祓, Great Purification)''' – December 31 === Ritual Performances === * '''Kagura Shishi''' – A sacred dance celebrating the emergence of the spring. * '''Shikadake Odori''' – A traditional dance. == Access == ; Nearest Station: * '''2-minute walk''' from '''{{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|en|Suita Station (Hankyu)|qid=Q7635540|lt=Suita Station (Hankyu)|1=Suita Station (Hankyu)}}''' on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|en|Hankyu Senri Line|qid=Q1044987|lt=Hankyu Senri Line|1=Hankyu Senri Line}}. ; Roads: * Near the intersection of '''{{ill|Japan National Route 479|en|Japan National Route 479|qid=Q6157657|lt=Japan National Route 479|1=Japan National Route 479}} (Osaka Inner Loop Line) and {{ill|Osaka Prefectural Route 14|ja|大阪府道14号大阪高槻京都線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefectural Route 14|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大阪府道・京都府道14号大阪高槻京都線|1=Osaka Prefectural Road and Kyoto Prefectural Road Route 14|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11441391}}'''. == Associated Shrines == Izudono-gū Shrine also manages the following shrines: * '''{{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Suita, Osaka)|ja|春日神社 (吹田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Suita, Osaka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579312|1=Kasuga Shrine (Suita, Osaka)}}''' – Kasuga, Suita[http://www.idudono.jp/yukarifile/kasugajinja.html Izudono-gū Shrine Official Website][https://yaokami.jp/1270219/ yaokami] * '''Senriyama Shrine (千里山神社)''' – Senriyama, Suita * '''Ōnoki Myōjin Shrine (大ノ木明神社)''' – Uchihonmachi, Suita * '''Ōtake Inari Shrine (大竹稲荷神社)''' – Kawagishi-chō, Suita == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Izudono-gū}} * [http://www.idudono.jp/index.html Izudono-gū Shrine Official Website] * [https://osaka-info.jp/page/idudonogu Izudono-gū Shrine | OSAKA-INFO] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|泉殿宮|version=101707246|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:泉殿宮]] {{Commons category|Izudono-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11554857}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suita, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Suita, Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
